questions.question: 1. What is the primary purpose of the VR classroom?

explanations.b: The VR classroom’s main purpose is to provide immersive, interactive learning experiences.

questions.question: 2. Which device is commonly used to interact in a VR classroom?

explanations.a: VR controllers allow participants to interact with virtual objects and answer questions.

questions.question: 3. In the VR classroom, questions can be presented in which formats?

explanations.b: VR classroom can include text, images, animations, and interactive components.

questions.question: 4. How are participant responses collected in the VR lab?

explanations.b: Responses are captured directly through the VR system, ensuring instant evaluation.

questions.question: 5. When can participants see correct answers in the VR classroom?

answers.b: Only at the end of the session

explanations.c: Participants can see correct answers immediately, providing real-time learning feedback.

questions.question: 6. What can the instructor do with performance data?

explanations.a: Instructors can use performance data to give personalized guidance and improve learning.

questions.question: 7. Which of these is NOT an advantage of the VR classroom?

explanations.c: VR classrooms reduce the need for physical maintenance, which is not an advantage.

questions.question: 8. What feature helps participants learn from their mistakes?

explanations.b: This feature helps learners understand mistakes and improve.
VR classrooms promote engagement and active learning, rather than passive listening.

questions.question: 10. Which component is essential for experiencing the VR lab?

explanations.a: A VR headset is essential to experience the virtual environment fully.
